Vendored QR code generator for the Direct Send tool (web/send/).
Fetched: 2026-07-08

## Library: qrcode-generator@1.4.4 (npm, via unpkg)

Source: https://unpkg.com/qrcode-generator@1.4.4/qrcode.js
License: MIT (see ./LICENSE — the package itself ships no separate LICENSE
  file on unpkg; the text above is the standard MIT license with the
  copyright holder taken from the header comment inside qrcode.js itself:
  "Copyright (c) 2009 Kazuhiko Arase").

Files:
  qrcode.js   56,694 bytes
    sha256: 18ae399f81182bc9de916e9c77b195df20cc58d6f2d55a62b085a299f1bf1780

This is the plain UMD build: loaded same-origin via a classic (non-module)
<script> tag in web/send/index.html, which defines a single global
`qrcode(typeNumber, errorCorrectionLevel)` factory function (no bundler,
no build step — matching how /vendor/pdfjs/ and /vendor/tesseract/ are
already vendored in this repo). Used only to render the offer link / reply
code as a scannable QR (via createSvgTag); never fetched from a third-party
CDN at runtime, and never sends any data anywhere — the QR is generated
entirely client-side from a string already held in memory.

Not vendored: qrcode.js also supports Node/CommonJS/AMD wiring in its UMD
footer (unused here) and there is no separate .min.js or TypeScript
declaration file published for this version.
